text:
Shafaqna Science | Farinaz Parnianifard-If you’ve been watching the Olympics, you’ve probably noticed dark, bruised circles on the backs of some of the swimmers. What are they? Do they help competitors or improve health? We take a look at cupping, which according to IFLScience and the Tribune, is the cause.

Cupping or “Hijama” is an ancient therapy that means “sucking” or “returning something to its normal state”. It is also the Sunnah or tradition of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) that this helps blood flow better, using cups to draw fluid into the skin and animal horns can also be used.

A vacuum pulls the skin up into the cups. This breaks the capillaries in the skin, causing the bruise-like effect seen on swimmers. Vacuum can be created with a flame or suction. The cups remain in place for 5 to 15 minutes. This draws more blood to the area, helping it to heal and break up scar tissue.

There are two types of cupping: wet and dry. In wet cupping, small cuts are made to draw blood or fluid from the body. Dry cupping does not draw blood, but can be combined with massage.

The Hadith (saying) of the Prophet (PBUH) as narrated by one of his companions, Jabir bin ‘Abdullah, emphasizes the importance of cupping.

Jabir bin ‘Abdullah said: “I will not leave until he is cupped, for I heard the Prophet (PBUH) say, ‘There is healing in cupping.'” [Source: Saheeh Bukhari: Volume 7, Book 71, Number 600].

In another Hadith, the Prophet (PBUH) said:“Healing is in three things: A gulp of honey, cupping, and branding with fire (cauterising). But I forbid my followers to use (cauterization) branding with fire.” [Source: Saheeh Bukhari Volume 7, Book 71, Number 584.]

Cupping is done in several stages. Cupping is usually done on the upper back between the shoulder blades. Cupping is the opposite of massage. The suction pulls the skin, tissues and muscles upward, according to Mind Body Green.

Cupping can help with many diseases, including anaemia, high blood pressure, skin problems, migraines, anxiety, depression, asthma, and more. It was also used to cure magic and poison because it cleanses your blood.

Source:IFLScienceandTribune
